voice: Bold, direct, second-person coaching that treats every session like the clock is running and you're the athlete.

designTokens.paletteMood: Blacked-out gym floor under a single neon-lime work light: heavy charcoal, one electric accent, nothing soft.

subtitle.value: IRONLINE is a small-group strength and conditioning gym in Bushwick, Brooklyn, running coached barbell and metabolic classes capped at 14 people so you always get hands on the bar.

items.label: Years coaching lifters on Flushing Ave
Classes on the schedule each week
Members who've earned their first pull-up here

data.title: Pick Your Lane, Then Show Up
data.subtitle: Every class is coached start to finish, scaled to whoever walks through the door, and built off a 12-week strength cycle so your numbers actually move.

items.title: IRONLINE Strength
items.description: Barbell-focused hour built on squat, press, deadlift and Olympic-lift variations. You'll follow a written 12-week progression and log every set so the weight climbs on purpose, not by feel.

items.title: Engine (Conditioning)
items.description: 45 minutes of rowing, assault-bike, and kettlebell intervals designed to raise your work capacity. Heart-rate zones are called out loud so you know when to push and when to breathe.

items.title: Hybrid 60
items.description: A blended session that pairs a heavy lower-body lift with a metabolic finisher. Built for members training for Hyrox, obstacle races, or just wanting to lift heavy and still gas out.

items.title: Foundations (Beginner)
items.description: A four-week onboarding block that teaches the hinge, squat, brace and barbell setup before you join open classes. No experience assumed, no ego allowed on the floor.

items.title: Open Gym
items.description: Two-hour unstructured blocks with a coach on the floor to spot, cue, and program-check. Use our platforms, rig, and specialty bars to hit accessory work on your own terms.

items.title: Mobility & Recovery
items.description: A Sunday-only 40-minute session of loaded stretching, breathwork, and soft-tissue work to keep your hips, shoulders, and ankles moving for the week ahead.

data.title: What 45 Minutes A Day Actually Does
data.subtitle: These are members who started where you're standing right now, most of them unsure they belonged in a barbell gym at all.

items.name: Marcus D.
items.quote: I came in unable to squat below parallel without my knees caving. Coach Renata rebuilt my setup in Foundations and eighteen months later I pulled 405 at a member meet. The programming is genuinely written, not made up on the spot.

items.name: Priya S.
items.quote: The 6am Engine class is the only reason I train consistently. It's 45 minutes, the coaches call your heart-rate zones out loud, and I'm at my desk by 7:30 having already done the hardest thing of my day.

items.name: Jordan T.
items.quote: I signed up eight weeks out from my first Hyrox. The Hybrid 60 sessions mapped almost exactly to race demands, and I finished under 90 minutes. Nobody else in Bushwick is programming like this.

items.name: Ana G.
items.quote: Fourteen people a class means the coach actually knows my name and my weak side. I've trained at big-box gyms for a decade and never once got corrected on my hinge until I came here.

data.title: No Contracts, No Initiation Fee
data.subtitle: Every membership includes coached classes, program tracking in the IRONLINE app, and open-gym access. Cancel any time with 30 days notice.

tiers.name: 2x Weekly
tiers.description: For the lifter building a habit around a busy week, guaranteed two coached sessions.
features: Full access to the IRONLINE training app
Freeze up to 30 days a year

data.title: Your First Week Is On Us
data.subtitle: Come train five classes over seven days, meet the coaches, and hit a real barbell before you spend a dollar. Bring the free-week pass to the Flushing Ave door.

title.value: Six Ways To Train. One Standard.

subtitle.value: Whether you're chasing a first pull-up or a 500-pound deadlift, every IRONLINE class is coached, scaled, and built off the same 12-week strength cycle running on the whiteboard.

data.title: A Typical Week On Flushing Ave
items: Monday: Strength (lower) at 6am, 12pm, 5:30pm, 6:45pm; Engine at 7am and 5:30pm
Tuesday: Strength (upper) at 6am, 12pm, 6pm; Foundations at 7pm; Open Gym 2-4pm
Wednesday: Hybrid 60 at 6am, 5:30pm, 6:45pm; Engine at 12pm; Mobility prep at 8pm
Thursday: Strength (full) at 6am, 12pm, 5:30pm; Engine at 6:45pm; Foundations at 7pm
Friday: Strength (lower) at 6am, 12pm, 5:30pm; Engine at 7am; Open Gym 3-5pm
Saturday: Hybrid 60 at 8am and 9:15am; Engine at 10:30am; Open Gym 11am-1pm
Sunday: Mobility & Recovery at 9am and 10am; Open Gym 11am-1pm

data.title: Not Sure Where To Start?
data.subtitle: Book a free 20-minute consult with a coach. We'll watch you move, talk goals, and put you in the right class, no pressure to sign anything.

title.value: Train Free For A Week. Decide After.

subtitle.value: Five classes, seven days, zero cost. Meet the coaches, load a real barbell, and see if IRONLINE is your gym before you ever put in a card.

data.title: Straight Answers, No Sales Pitch
data.subtitle: The questions we get at the front desk on Flushing Ave every single week.

items.question: I've never touched a barbell. Am I going to embarrass myself?
items.answer: No. Everyone starts in Foundations, a four-week onboarding block that teaches the squat, hinge, and barbell setup before you join open classes. Coaches scale every movement, and classes cap at 14 so you're never lost in a crowd.

items.question: How much does membership cost and is there a contract?
items.answer: Memberships run $99 to $219 a month with no contract and no initiation fee. You can freeze or cancel any time with 30 days notice, and your first week is completely free.

items.question: Where exactly are you and is there parking?
items.answer: We're on Flushing Ave in Bushwick, Brooklyn, a six-minute walk from the Flushing Ave M station. Street parking is free after 6pm, and there's a paid lot on Bogart Street two blocks away.

items.question: What are your hours?
items.answer: Coached classes run 6am to 8pm weekdays and 8am to 1pm on weekends, with open-gym blocks layered in. Sunday is mobility and recovery only, from 9am to 1pm.

items.question: Do I need to be in shape before I start?
items.answer: Absolutely not. Roughly a third of our members had never lifted before joining. The whole point of coached, scaled classes is that we meet you exactly where you are and progress you from there.

items.question: How does the free week work?
items.answer: Book online, show up, and train up to five classes over seven days at no cost. You'll get a full Foundations-style intro on your first visit, meet the coaches, and try every program style before deciding anything.

items.question: Can I freeze my membership if I travel or get injured?
items.answer: Yes. Every membership includes up to 30 freeze days a year, and we extend that with a doctor's note for injury or medical leave. Just email the front desk 48 hours before you want the freeze to start.

items.question: Do you have showers and lockers?
items.answer: Yes, two full locker rooms with showers, towel service, and day-use lockers. Bring your own lock or grab a rental at the desk for two dollars.

data.title: Find Us On Flushing Ave
data.subtitle: Walk in, watch a class, and talk to a coach, no appointment needed during open-gym hours.

data.title: Stop Reading. Start Lifting.
data.subtitle: Your free week is waiting. Book it now, show up on Flushing Ave, and find out what 45 focused minutes a day actually does.

metadata.structureNotes: Home deliberately front-loads a palette:primary stats banner immediately after the hero to establish authority through numbers before selling programs, and inserts a tabBar nav strip mid-page instead of ending on the generic services->testimonials->cta flow, giving the dense energetic pacing the brief asked for.
